--- title: "[viz.get_risk_table_frame()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Return a tidy frame of the number at risk per stratum at each of `times`. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/viz/_shared.py#L63-L98){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python viz.get_risk_table_frame(km, times=None, *, format=None) ``` ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [km]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [KaplanMeier]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A fitted `KaplanMeier` estimator. [times]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Query times for the numbers-at-risk table. Defaults to an automatic grid. [format]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Output format: `None` (default), `"pandas"`, `"polars"`, or `"pyarrow"`. When `None`, a backend is auto-detected (Polars, then Pandas, then PyArrow). ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [DataFrame]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A tidy frame with columns `strata`, `time`, and `n_risk` (one row per stratum per time point). ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} Fit a stratified Kaplan-Meier estimator on the bundled `lung` dataset, then tabulate the number of subjects still at risk in each group at a chosen set of times. This returns the numbers as a tidy frame (one row per stratum and time). ::: {#f69ccffd .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) km = gw.KaplanMeier().fit(y, by=lung["sex"]) gw.get_risk_table_frame(km, times=[0, 250, 500, 750, 1000], format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
shape: (10, 3)
stratatimen_risk
strf64f64

"2"1000.00.0
``` ::: :::
